notes: opencode streams assistant output as incremental text parts; parseTextPart maps assistant text to assistant_message (no turn boundary, excluded from IsAgentDone) and step-finish reason=tool-calls also stays working — only step-finish reason=stop emits turn_done. The WAL-fsnotify ProcessOwnedStore watcher observes the SQLite part rows for the ses_… session, so partial flushes that reach the WAL across multiple scan windows do not flicker to ready. Driver: a single send producing a longer multi-line reply (opencode run blocks; wait_turn no-op). OBSERVABILITY RISK to confirm at record time: a no-tool prose turn run headlessly can flush all assistant text + the terminal step-finish to the WAL in one scan window (basic-turn collapsed working→ready in ~0.3ms), giving the streaming_window nothing intermediate to assert; if so this is an observability gap to surface, not a spec rebase.

sources.excerpt: parseTextPart assistant -> assistant_message (no turn boundary); only step-finish reason=stop -> turn_done
WAL-fsnotify-driven scanParts: part rows with time_updated >= cursor emitted as EventActivity
